optional_inputs.notes: Native clients may consume live backend responses only after Live Backend Handoff Contract policies and a hosted backend exist.
Relay integration remains optional and future; no relay runtime exists.
Production signed snapshots require signing/key policy and release provenance before native clients can claim authenticity.

prohibited_behaviors_v0: production signing or real key material
required_verification_behaviors: verify native GUI implemented flags remain false
verify CLI is described as the current local native-like surface
verify public data and snapshot consumer dependencies exist
verify future GUI lanes remain future, deferred, or lab-verify
verify no Visual Studio or Xcode project files are introduced
verify install, download, and execution automation remain policy-blocked
verify Rust remains parity-only and unwired
allowed_actions_future: search, browse, and inspect public results
open or import static snapshots after consumer contract review
maintain local cache after privacy policy exists
hand off to package managers or installers after action, rights, and security policy exists
integrate with a relay after relay prototype planning and operator signoff
disallowed_actions_until_policy: upload logs or diagnostics by default

action_policy_dependency.notes: Native Action / Download / Install Policy v0 is required before any native download, install handoff, package-manager handoff, mirror, execute, restore, uninstall, or rollback behavior.

local_cache_privacy_policy_dependency.notes: Native Local Cache / Privacy Policy v0 is required before any native local cache, private cache, telemetry, diagnostics upload, accounts, cloud sync, or private file ingestion behavior.

public_data_dependency.notes: Native clients may read static public data summaries, but must not treat them as a live production API.

rust_dependency_status.notes: Rust source-registry/query-planner/local-index work remains isolated parity or planning and is not a native SDK or FFI dependency.

python_oracle_status.notes: Future clients consume governed public data, snapshot, API, or relay contracts rather than engine internals.

required_capabilities: open a snapshot directory only after following the snapshot consumer contract
display evidence and provenance without rewriting truth

future_capabilities_after_policy: use a local cache only after Native Local Cache / Privacy Policy v0 and implementation review
integrate with a local relay sidecar
hand off to installers or package managers only after action/download/install policy, security, rights/access, user-confirmation, and implementation work exists

notes: Native Client Contract v0 is design, inventory, validation, and documentation only.

No Visual Studio project, Xcode project, native GUI app, native snapshot reader runtime, relay, FFI, installer automation, package-manager behavior, download automation, execution automation, malware scanning, rights clearance, live source probe, or Rust runtime wiring is implemented.
CLI remains the only current native-like surface.
Future native clients are super-clients over governed public data, snapshots, live handoff, and relay contracts, not website wrappers and not alternative truth engines.
